blocks.content: Shirone gathered his friends and explained the plan.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: After hearing it, Neid and Iruki fell silent.
Sensing their lack of confidence, Shirone added,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It doesn't have to be exactly this way, but doing this will increase our chances, right? For example, using keywords or…
Iruki shook his head.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: No, Shirone, it's fine. More than fine—it's bound to succeed. This is quite profound. With this method, we can use the presentation as foreshadowing.
Once the big picture was clear, Shirone and his friends began detailing the plan.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The remaining problem is this: how do we create a ghost?
The technical part was Neid's specialty.
Rising from the sofa, he cast Lightning Shadow, and an electrical afterimage of himself appeared.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: With Lightning Shadow, I can create a realistic ghostly form. But no one would fall for this. It can't move, for one.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then let's think from there. How do we make the ghostly form move?
The first thing that comes to mind is this: to make Lightning Shadow move, you can cast the spell at an incredibly high speed.
Neid cast Lightning Shadow repeatedly while walking.
Though disjointed, the afterimage appeared to be walking.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I see. So, we can use optical illusions to make it seem real?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Right. But magic alone has limits. Plus, at my level, I can't increase the number of entities.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How about using mechanical devices?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: It's not as easy as it sounds. Even if we make the device, there's a mountain of things to consider. The biggest problem is that just moving won't fool anyone. To truly look like a ghost, it needs to react to external stimuli.
Neid, who had once built a hologram device, knew creating a ghostly illusion wasn't the issue.
But if it couldn't react to people's actions, they'd quickly see through it.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: What if we predict people's reactions in advance?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Predict reactions?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Apply complex systems theory. Analyze the behavioral patterns of the crowd and program them. Make them think they're getting feedback. But the amount of information is enormous. We'd need a medium to transmit the data and a control device.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Hmm. Data transmission. My electrical power definitely has limits. Then… ah!

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Photon Output! Shirone's Photon Output!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah. Photon Output…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now it's coming together. Neid will build the hologram device. I'll write the program for it. Shirone's Photon Output will transmit my program to the device. It'll require massive output—can you do it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll make it happen. This is our chance.
It was a ray of light in the darkness.
Just having hope for what seemed impossible moved Neid deeply.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We can do this. We can save the research club. Assuming nothing goes wrong, of course.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If we succeed, the school will be turned upside down, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: More than that. It'll be chaos. I'm all in. Plus, it sounds fun. Hehe. I can't wait to see the teachers' reactions.
Shirone and his friends exchanged meaningful smiles.
With the fate of the Supernatural Parapsychology Research Club at stake, an unprecedented event in the magic school's history was about to unfold.
Shirone devoted his time to increasing the power of his Photon Output.
The key was to boost its intensity and duration.
Since photons had no mass and couldn't exert physical force, they couldn't be used as offensive magic, but this very trait became an advantage now.
He could train in any of Istar's storage rooms.
Shirone raised his concentration with a magical action, extending both hands, and cast Photon Output.
A beam of light spread in a circle on the wall.
First was duration. If he couldn't sustain it for 10 minutes, increasing the output was a pipe dream.
Maintaining Photon Output for 10 minutes was beyond Class Five, but the same went for Neid's hologram device and Iruki's complex systems equations.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I have to do this. I have to…
Shirone gritted his teeth and held on.
After 3 minutes, his consciousness began to blur.
During the speed gun test, even a single burst of Photon Output had barely lasted a minute.
Compared to that, this was remarkable growth, but it still fell far short of the goal.
Gah! Huff!
The time he managed to hold on with all his might was 3 minutes and 20 seconds.
He had to increase this to 10 minutes within 15 days.
After recording the time, he rested for exactly 20 minutes.
Neglecting rest in such training could be life-threatening.
As Shirone slumped down, he noticed the noise from the next room.
Neid seemed to have started welding.
During his break, he decided to check on him.
Opening the door, he saw Neid buried in various parts.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey, you're here? How's the output time?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: So-so. Increased by 20 seconds. How about you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: As you can see, still working on the engine.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Can you make it in time?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: I have to. Don't worry. Prototypes always take time. Once production starts, fixing errors becomes hard. That's why we take our time to be thorough.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I see. By the way, what about what Iruki mentioned?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Oh, that? Got it yesterday. Here.
Neid threw a finger-thick cord at Shirone's feet.
The outer layer was rubber, and inside was a transparent membrane like quartz.
It was something Iruki had specially requested from his father, the head of the Thunder Dragon faction.
With 300 hologram devices to be installed around the school, it was impossible for Shirone to cast Photon Output on all of them alone.
At first, Neid thought of mirrors.
Shirone would cast Photon Output, and the light would reflect off mirrors to reach the devices.
But in reality, the light would bend too much.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Neid wondered if more mirrors would work.
By attaching mirrors to small hoses, he could create tunnels for the light, mimicking the effect of light bending as if differentiating a straight line.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Proudly, Neid announced this. If they could make it, they'd be rolling in money, he added.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: But Iruki shattered his excitement.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: His father's monthly letters often included updates on magical society, and a few months ago, they'd described something identical to Neid's idea—called 'optical fibers.'
Neid was utterly disappointed.
Even in the ivory towers of magic's cutting edge, this was a newly developed field, so even Iruki felt it was a waste.
But upon analyzing the actual fibers, Neid shook his head.
The engineering was so delicate it was beyond his current skill.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'Still, I thought of the same thing as the ivory tower. Neid, you're amazing.'
Shirone picked up the optical fiber and examined it closely.
To the naked eye, it didn't seem special.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: But you managed to get it.
Neid, having finished welding, rubbed his reddened eyes and stood.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: The ivory tower is still in the early stages, so the performance isn't great. Let's test it first. I made a tester.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Neid handed the modified optical fiber to Shirone.
Unlike before, it had a transparent orb attached at the end.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What do I do with this?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Just hold it and cast Photon Output. I'll measure the efficiency. Pour everything into it—we need the maximum output.
Since his break was over, Shirone didn't hold back.
As light gathered in his hand, the orb glowed faintly.
The needle on Neid's connected device began to sway.
After watching the gauge until the 3-minute limit, Neid spoke dejectedly.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Sigh, Iruki was right. This is a total early-stage prototype. Well, I should be grateful we even have this much.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That bad?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Efficiency is only 18%. Only 18% of the light you send reaches the destination.
Shirone also felt hollow.
With maintaining Photon Output for 10 minutes already a distant goal, 18% efficiency meant he'd need to increase his output fivefold just to match 100%.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Shirone, can you do it? If you can't increase the output, we'll never succeed. High-output magic energy is impossible to achieve with machines alone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll try. Practice will make it possible.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: You're sure you can do it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yeah. Don't worry. I'll figure it out. I'll go practice now.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I said I got it!
Shirone lay sprawled in the storage room, gasping for breath.
Half a day of Photon Output had taken its toll, both mentally and physically.
Huff! Huff!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Still, it's nowhere near enough. How can I reach 10 minutes?
Maybe it was impossible from the start.
Photon Output, being a sustained spell, drained mental energy far faster than single-action spells.
Maintaining it for 10 minutes was something only pros could do.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh, I don't know. I'll try again tomorrow when I'm fresh.
Neid opened the storage room door and entered.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Shirone, done?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yeah. Can't go on today. Need to rest.
I'm exhausted too from just sparks. It's past midnight. Let's head back to Iruki.
Time had flown.
Shirone had spent over 7 hours on Photon Output alone—a life-threatening amount of training.
If the school found out, they'd surely ban him from magic practice.
Shirone struggled to his feet.
Neid looked no better—his eyes were sunken, and his face was red from prolonged exposure to electricity.
Back at the research club, Iruki was swiftly moving his pen over the school's blueprint, calculating potential variables for the day of the operation.
Neid watched worriedly.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Like Shirone and himself, Iruki hadn't moved since sitting down.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: How's it going, Iruki? Calculations okay?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: So-so. We'll make it in time, so don't worry.
Neid's heart ached.
His ambition was hurting his friends.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Was it too much to ask? Calculating the entire school's behavioral patterns…

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I can do it. I said I can. Don't distract me. I'm busy.
With that, Iruki fell silent.
Equations filled the notebook at a terrifying speed.
